name: Technicolor
summary: Replaces the elementary Higgs scalar with a new strongly-coupled gauge sector ('technicolor') whose chiral condensate spontaneously breaks SU(2) x U(1). The W and Z masses come from the eaten technipions; the Higgs scalar is a composite resonance. Eliminates the hierarchy problem by removing fundamental scalars altogether but struggles with fermion mass generation (extended technicolor, walking technicolor) and electroweak precision (S parameter).

addresses_puzzles.note: Removes the fundamental Higgs and its quadratic mass divergence; the EW scale is generated dynamically by dimensional transmutation. Vanilla QCD-like technicolor is excluded by EW precision (S parameter) and by the discovery of a 125 GeV scalar with SM-like couplings; walking and conformal technicolor variants survive in narrower windows.

touches_benchmarks.note: Vanilla technicolor predicts a heavy resonance spectrum and not a 125 GeV scalar with SM couplings - excluded by LHC. Walking / conformal variants with a light dilaton evade this in part.
QCD-like technicolor predicts S ~ 0.3, in stark conflict with measured S ~ 0; walking dynamics can suppress S but require careful tuning.
Extended technicolor needed for fermion masses generates FCNCs at 4-fermion level; K-Kbar mixing is the tightest constraint.

new_fields: extended technicolor gauge bosons (in ETC variants)
introduces.note: TC gauge group, condensate scale, ETC scale and structure for fermion masses, walking dynamics parameters.

history: Susskind (1979), 'Dynamics of spontaneous symmetry breaking in the Weinberg-Salam theory', Phys. Rev. D 20, 2619; Weinberg (1979), 'Implications of dynamical symmetry breaking', Phys. Rev. D 19, 1277. Extended technicolor for fermion masses (Dimopoulos-Susskind 1979). LHC's discovery of a 125 GeV SM-like Higgs in 2012 retired vanilla technicolor as a leading framework; walking and conformal variants and partial-compositeness descendants continue.

references.citation: Susskind, 'Dynamics of spontaneous symmetry breaking in the Weinberg-Salam theory', Phys. Rev. D 20, 2619 (1979)
Weinberg, 'Implications of dynamical symmetry breaking', Phys. Rev. D 19, 1277 (1979)
